title: Microchilus sparreorum

This plant is further classified into the subfamily Apostasioideae, a small and ancient lineage within the Orchidaceae. Microchilus sparreorum is a species of orchid that is found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. intro: Microchilus sparreorum, commonly known as Sparre's orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Microchilus sparreorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
